auth_notes: Requires Client ID and Client Secret to obtain a Bearer token via the /token endpoint. Some endpoints also require an X-Availity-Customer-ID header.
rate_limits: Varies by API and subscription tier; typically enforced per second and per month based on the specific API product.
gotchas: Strict HIPAA compliance requirements mean data must be handled securely within the n8n environment.
The API uses complex healthcare-specific data structures (JSON representations of X12 EDI) which can be difficult to map.
Access to specific payers or features often requires additional manual approval within the Availity portal.
research_notes: Integration will require a two-step HTTP Request process: first to the OAuth2 token endpoint to retrieve an access token, followed by the actual API call using that token in the Authorization header. Use n8n's 'OAuth2' credential type or a 'Wait' node/sub-workflow to manage token expiration if high volume is expected.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will require a two-step HTTP Request process: first to the OAuth2 token endpoint to retrieve an access token, followed by the actual API call using that token in the Authorization header. Use n8n's 'OAuth2' credential type or a 'Wait' node/sub-workflow to manage token expiration if high volume is expected.
